metadata.title: Cardbound MUD
metadata.description: A Saturday-morning modern city where Duel Monsters, Pokemon, Magic spells, Gunpla frames, One Piece crews, and Union Arena crossovers burst out of cards and cause spectacularly manageable havoc.

zones.name: Duelist Downtown
zones.description: Battle City has leaked into the bus schedule: Duel Disks spark between buses, KaibaCorp-looking signs blink over snack kiosks, and every crosswalk feels like a declared Battle Phase.

zones.name: Neon Battle Mall
zones.description: A neon indoor maze where Game Corner cabinets, Duel Terminal screens, judge calls, and five-color Magic leaks keep turning errands into side events.

zones.name: Poke-Gunpla Park
zones.description: A split park-and-toy district where Pokemon rescue tags hang from benches, Gunpla runners cover the grass, and every whistle sounds like a starting signal.

zones.name: Union Harbor
zones.description: The waterfront convention grounds where Straw Hat banners, Union Arena AP counters, guest-star stages, and finale monsters all insist they are the main event.

doors.name: Duel Disk turnstile
doors.description: A mall turnstile wired to accept a Duel Disk keycard before it lets anyone into Battle City Jr.

doors.name: White Base hangar door
doors.description: A rolling hangar door with a toy-sized catapult rail bolted across it.

doors.name: Union Arena gate
doors.description: A convention-stage gate made of clashing card borders, AP counters, and bad timing.

quests.name: Duel Disk Lockdown
quests.summary: Open the mall route, log your first Collection card, and make sure the information kiosk survived first contact with living Duel Monsters.
quests.description: Marshal Echo needs the emergency Duel Disk keycard from the card shop. Log a Collection card, get the keycard, beat the Blue-Eyes White Dragon back from the turnstiles, reach the concourse, and check in with Milo at the kiosk.

steps.label: Learn from Marshal Echo why the mall gate needs the Duel Disk keycard.
steps.objective: Ask Marshal Echo about the starter kit.

steps.label: Take the Duel Disk keycard from Sleeve Queen Card Shop.
Log your first Collection card by defeating a runaway card monster.
Unlock the Duel Disk turnstile.
Open the Duel Disk turnstile.
Defeat Blue-Eyes White Dragon at the turnstiles.
Reach Neon Mall Concourse.
Talk to Kiosk Kid Milo.

quests.name: Rotom Radio Relay
quests.summary: Help the food court radio crew broadcast monster warnings across Cardbound City after your binder proves you can handle the route.
quests.description: Signal DJ Pax can warn the city if you have at least three Collection cards, gather a Rotom antenna sticker, retrieve the rooftop signal booster, defeat the Fan Rotom, and tell Bea to fire up the mast.

steps.label: Learn Pax's broadcast plan.
Grab the Rotom antenna sticker from the transit platform.
Retrieve the Rotom signal booster from the broadcast roof.
Reach the Broadcast Roof.
Defeat the Fan Rotom before it scrambles the relay.
Tell Roof Technician Bea the Rotom relay is ready.

quests.name: Poke Ball Roundup
quests.summary: Help Ranger Mira calm the small Pokemon-adjacent monsters before they learn capitalism from the hot dog cart.
quests.description: The park monsters are scared, hungry, and fast. Build your binder to five cards, gather Poke Ball rescue tags, check Pocket Park, calm the Misty's Gyarados, and report back to Mira.

steps.label: Learn Ranger Mira's rescue plan.
Pick up the Poke Ball rescue tags.
Reach Pocket Park before the little ones raid the hot dog cart.
Defeat Misty's Gyarados before the park turns into a Water-type gym.
Report back to Ranger Mira.

quests.name: Mana Leak Mayhem
quests.summary: Sort out the pop-up Magic lab before the colors start making policy decisions.
quests.description: Professor Amper needs a seven-card binder sample, a Mox prism shard, and a vault check to stabilize the mall's uncontained mana before Nicol Bolas finishes monologuing.

steps.label: Ask Professor Amper about the prism leak.
Take the Mox prism shard from the service hall.
Reach the Prism Vault before the color pie starts voting.
Defeat Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God before it resolves another Elder Dragon Scheme.
Take the prism badge from the vault.
Return to Professor Amper.

quests.name: White Base Launch Trouble
quests.summary: Get into the Gunpla hangar and stop the model kits from inventing dramatic launch procedures.
quests.description: Mechanic Joss needs a nine-card threat sample and the Gunpla servo key recovered from the training lot. Unlock the White Base hangar door, enter the hangar, and defeat the model kits inventing dramatic launch procedures.

steps.label: Ask Mechanic Joss about the frame bay.
Recover the Gunpla servo key from the training lot.
Unlock the White Base hangar door.
Open the White Base hangar door.
Reach the Snap-Frame Hangar.
Defeat Full Armor Gundam on the catapult rail.
Defeat Char's Zaku II before it goes three times faster through the hangar.
Talk to Mechanic Joss in the hangar.

quests.name: Union Arena Calamity
quests.summary: Open the final pavilion and stop every series from resolving its finale at the same time.
quests.description: Coordinator Vale has the Union Arena pass, the stage has the gate, and the pavilion has too many Final Triggers. Bring a twelve-card binder, beat the Meruem, open the gate, and face the Final Trigger Titan.

steps.label: Ask Coordinator Vale about the crossover.
Take the Union Arena pass from Coordinator Vale.
Defeat the Meruem before opening the finale route.
Unlock the Union Arena gate.
Open the Union Arena gate.
Reach the Crossover Pavilion.
Defeat Final Trigger Titan in the Crossover Pavilion.
Tell Captain Rook the finale route is open.

rooms.name: Cardbound Plaza
rooms.description: Emergency cones, folding tables, Duel Disk chargers, and glowing booster wrappers fill the plaza. A giant sign blinks: KEEP CALM, DECLARE YOUR PHASES, AND DO NOT SUMMON IN TRAFFIC.

rooms.name: Sleeve Queen Card Shop
rooms.description: Binders lie open like emergency manuals. A Duel Terminal near the counter keeps asking if anyone would like to Normal Summon responsibly, and a glass case labeled NOT FOR THE APOCALYPSE is already empty.

rooms.name: Side Deck Alley
rooms.description: A narrow alley glittering with penny sleeves, runaway dice, and KaibaCorp stickers. Someone chalked SIDE DECK HERE on the pavement and the pavement took that personally.

rooms.name: Duel Disk Turnstiles
rooms.description: The mall entrance is blocked by Duel Disk turnstiles. Each scanner hums like it is waiting for a dramatic wrist flick. A huge white dragon is coiled around the scanners; the turnstiles feel less like a checkpoint and more like the boss fight guarding the route.

south.blockedMessage: The Duel Disk turnstile flashes red. It wants the Duel Disk keycard from the card shop.

rooms.name: Food Court Barricade
rooms.description: Pretzel carts form a defensive wall. Someone has written DO NOT FEED THE BLUE-EYES on a napkin dispenser, then underlined BLUE-EYES three times.

rooms.name: Rotom Transit Platform
rooms.description: The buses have stopped, but their route signs keep flipping between Gym badges, boss-monster names, and one Rotom face that keeps rating everyone two stars.

rooms.name: Neon Mall Concourse
rooms.description: Escalators move on their own, ad screens shout combo advice, and Kiosk Kid Milo is using receipt paper to track every Duel Monster, Pokemon, and suspicious coupon knight sighting.

rooms.name: Game Corner Arcade
rooms.description: Cabinets beep in panic. A claw machine has captured three tiny monsters and somehow elected itself Game Corner champion.

rooms.name: Rooftop Stairs
rooms.description: Emergency lights chase each other up the stairwell while Magic card borders peel off the walls like stickered enchantments. Someone has written PLEASE HOLD PRIORITY on the handrail.

rooms.name: Rotom Broadcast Roof
rooms.description: Rotom has possessed the rooftop ventilation fan beside a pirate radio mast built from selfie sticks and foil dividers. Charged gusts scramble the signal whenever Fan Rotom gets excited.

rooms.name: Judge Call Service Hall
rooms.description: A staff-only corridor has become a rulebook-only corridor. The vending machines accept coins, cards, and sincere apologies to the table judge.

north.blockedMessage: The mall lockdown shutters are tied to the Duel Disk turnstile keycard.

rooms.name: Pop-Up Magic Lab
rooms.description: A science fair booth has grown into a five-color Magic lab of safety goggles, Mox shards, and equations ending in several exclamation marks. The whiteboard says DO NOT TAP THE PRISM.

rooms.name: Color Pie Vault
rooms.description: A former storage room now contains a floating prism that sorts uncontained mana into colors, then immediately argues with itself about color identity. A Nicol Bolas standee watches the prism from the back wall with the smug stillness of an optional rare fight.

rooms.name: Gunpla Toy Aisle
rooms.description: Action figures, Gunpla runners, and a Hunter x Hunter Union Arena guest display stand beside half-empty shelves. Haro rolls between the boxes while Gon Freecss examines every model kit like newly discovered wildlife.

east.blockedMessage: The mall lockdown shutters are tied to the Duel Disk turnstile keycard.

rooms.name: Union Arena Preview Stage
rooms.description: Jujutsu Kaisen, Hunter x Hunter, and Code Geass banners crowd the Union Arena preview stage. AP counters climb on their own as Mahito circles the wings and Meruem waits beneath the main spotlight.

south.blockedMessage: The crossover gate needs a pass stamped by every genre station.

rooms.name: Poke Ball Park
rooms.description: A duck pond has been covered with floating Poke Ball rescue tags. Tiny monsters chase frisbees while volunteers pretend the tall grass is not spreading. The pond keeps sloshing like a Gym Leader is waiting under the surface, so only confident Trainers linger too close.

rooms.name: Pokemon Ranger Card Table
rooms.description: A ranger table is covered with clipboards, water bottles, and a hand-drawn chart titled CUTE DOES NOT MEAN SAFE TO PET. A Pokedex phone keeps buzzing anyway.

rooms.name: Gunpla Training Lot
rooms.description: Parking spaces have been repainted as mobile-suit lanes. Cones, chalk arrows, and one very stern whistle keep the Gunpla chaos almost organized.

west.blockedMessage: The frame bay door needs the servo key from the training lot.

rooms.name: White Base Snap-Frame Hangar
rooms.description: Gundam frames hang above a White Base-style catapult rail. Full Armor Gundam blocks the launch lane while Char's Zaku II circles the warning signs at three times the approved speed.

rooms.name: Union Harbor Walk
rooms.description: Food trucks, convention booths, and emergency tape line the waterfront. The bay reflects monsters that are not actually above it yet, which everyone agrees is rude.

north.blockedMessage: The harbor-side hangar shutter needs the Gunpla servo key.

rooms.name: Straw Hat Captain Pier
rooms.description: A One Piece booth spills across the pier in barrels, Jolly Roger banners, and impossible stories from Usopp. Dracule Mihawk waits at the far end with Yoru across one shoulder.

north.blockedMessage: The pavilion perimeter is sealed until the Union Arena pass clears the crossover gate.

rooms.name: Union Arena Pavilion
rooms.description: The final pavilion is a storm of card frames, Gunpla parts, snack wrappers, AP counters, and heroic speeches arriving out of order. The stage manager has given up and started tracking triggers.

items.name: Duel Disk keycard
items.description: A laminated KaibaCorp-looking keycard that smells faintly of fresh booster packs.

items.name: Rare Candy soda
items.description: A neon can promising focus, sparkle, and a level-up you should probably not trust.

items.name: Potion snack cake
items.description: A snack cake wrapped in shiny foil. Restores morale, Energy, and questionable confidence.

items.name: Rotom antenna sticker
items.description: A lightning-bolt sticker that makes any broadcast feel like a Rotom Phone emergency alert.

items.name: Rotom signal booster
items.description: A taped-together booster made from selfie sticks, foil rares, and suspiciously helpful Rotom static.

items.name: Poke Ball rescue tags
items.description: Soft plastic Poke Ball tags for marking panicked monsters as rescued instead of rampaging.

items.name: Mox prism shard
items.description: A rainbow-edged shard that hums in five colors and argues like a Magic table with no judge nearby.

items.name: Color Pie badge
items.description: A safety badge proving you survived the lab without becoming a five-color commander deck.

items.name: Gunpla servo key
items.description: A tiny motor key with a tag reading DO NOT CATAPULT LAUNCH INDOORS.

items.name: Straw Hat token
items.description: A plastic coin stamped with a smiling skull and the absolute certainty that the crew is coming.

items.name: Union Arena pass
items.description: A convention badge stamped by multiple series stations and one confused pretzel vendor.

items.name: Union Arena finale binder
items.description: A reinforced binder with dividers labeled Plans A through Saturday Morning Crossover.

items.name: Duel Disk holster
items.description: A hip holster for quick dramatic Duel Disk reveals and completely unnecessary pointing.

items.name: Pokemon Trainer jacket
items.description: A bright jacket with snack pockets, Poke Ball tag loops, and bite-resistant sleeves.

items.name: Trainer cap
items.description: A cap turned exactly the right amount backward for Pokemon-battle confidence.

items.name: Running Shoes
items.description: Scuffed Running Shoes with excellent grip on mall tile and questionable reality.

items.name: Planeswalker mana pin
items.description: A little enamel pin that changes color whenever Magic rules are nearby.

items.name: Gundam pilot gloves
items.description: Fingerless gloves for pilots, Gunpla builders, and people about to press the big red launch button.

items.name: Union Arena guest-star sticker
items.description: A holographic sticker that looks like it belongs to six different shows and somehow all of them count.

items.name: Secret Rare foil stamp
items.description: A tiny holographic stamp handed out by the event booth for catching limited-print troublemakers.

items.name: Millennium Puzzle replica
items.description: A heavy gold puzzle on a chain. It probably will not house an ancient spirit, but it absolutely improves dramatic pauses.

items.name: Trap Card sleeves
items.description: Purple-backed sleeves that make every set card look like it has been waiting all episode.

items.name: Pokedex phone
items.description: A red phone that identifies monsters, judges your walking pace, and keeps asking if you remembered to save.

items.name: Gym Badge pin
items.description: A shiny badge pin that makes helpful monsters listen slightly harder and unhelpful monsters pose for longer.

items.name: Black Lotus proxy
items.description: A very fake, very powerful-looking proxy that still makes every Magic player in the room inhale sharply.

items.name: Planeswalker deckbox
items.description: A sturdy deckbox with five-color dividers and a sticky note reading DO NOT SHUFFLE DURING AN EARTHQUAKE.

items.name: Beam rifle keychain
items.description: A tiny beam rifle charm that makes lock-on beeps whenever someone says this is probably safe.

items.name: Haro backpack
items.description: A round green backpack with little ears, too many pockets, and a surprising amount of emotional support.

items.name: Straw Hat sash
items.description: A red sash that makes every walk toward danger feel like the start of a crew speech.

items.name: Devil Fruit gummy
items.description: A swirly gummy that tastes like courage, bad decisions, and absolutely no swimming afterward.

items.name: Union Arena AP counter
items.description: A clicky counter that tracks AP, panic, and how many guest stars have entered from stage left.

items.name: Final Trigger lanyard
items.description: A convention lanyard that flashes whenever the scene is about to become everyone else's problem.

items.name: Chain Link token
items.description: A metal token stamped CL2 that clicks whenever you interrupt someone at exactly the dramatic moment.

items.name: Trainer buddy band
items.description: A soft wristband with little Poke Ball stitching, useful for staying calm when your partner bolts into trouble.

items.name: Planeswalker spark pin
items.description: A five-color pin that flickers like a tiny planeswalker spark whenever the stack gets argumentative.

items.name: White Base launch patch
items.description: An embroidered patch reading CATAPULT CLEAR, which is comforting right up until someone asks what a catapult is doing indoors.

items.name: Straw Hat crew charm
items.description: A small charm shaped like a smiling skull, warm from too many speeches about never leaving the crew behind.

items.name: Union Arena AP die
items.description: A chunky six-sided AP die that lands on exactly the number needed for a guest-star entrance suspiciously often.

items.name: KaibaCorp Duel Visor
items.description: A hard-light visor that highlights chain windows, attack lines, and every chance to say exactly as planned.

items.name: Rotom Running Shoes
items.description: Running Shoes with tiny Rotom displays in the soles, buzzing whenever a partner needs you there five seconds ago.

items.name: Manaweave sleeves
items.description: Five-color sleeves stitched into a light jacket liner for Planeswalkers who want their mana curve where they can see it.

items.name: Magnetic ankle boosters
items.description: Miniature Gundam thrusters that lock to your shoes and make every dodge feel like a catapult launch.

items.name: Captain's straw sandals
items.description: Simple sandals that somehow make every sprint toward trouble feel like the next island is already in sight.

items.name: Union judge headset
items.description: A headset with a push-to-talk button, a tiny AP display, and the authority to say rewind that trigger stack.

items.name: Max Potion soda
items.description: A fizzy blue bottle from the food court cooler. It tastes like victory, medicine, and getting back in the fight.

items.name: Judge Call espresso
items.description: A tiny coffee can with a warning label reading priority responses may occur. Restores a sharp burst of Energy.

items.name: Duel Academy headband
items.description: A red academy headband that makes every draw phase feel like finals week with a crowd chanting from the bleachers.

items.name: Ash's League Cap
items.description: A well-loved league cap with a bent brim, partner-monster bite marks, and impossible comeback energy stitched into the band.

items.name: Char custom visor
items.description: A red-tinted pilot visor that makes target locks feel faster, sharper, and at least three times more dramatic.

items.name: Luffy's straw hat
items.description: A straw hat with a red band and the terrifying emotional weight of an entire crew believing you can handle this.

items.name: KaibaCorp blazer
items.description: A sharp white blazer with glowing lapels, dramatic shoulder lines, and absolutely no tolerance for sloppy chain timing.

items.name: Team Rocket hoodie
items.description: A black hoodie with a red R, deep snack pockets, and the suspicious confidence to improvise when the plan detonates.

items.name: Planeswalker cloak
items.description: A travel-worn cloak with five-color threadwork and enough pockets for dice, sleeves, tokens, and one bad idea on the stack.

items.name: Union Arena staff vest
items.description: A neon staff vest covered in crossover pins, laminated rulings, and a radio that only speaks in trigger windows.

items.name: Duel Runner boots
items.description: Armored riding boots built for sprinting through Battle City traffic like someone just announced Riding Duel acceleration.

items.name: Acro Bike shoes
items.description: Springy shoes with scuffed soles, perfect for hopping curbs, dodging tiny monsters, and pretending stairs are optional.

items.name: Swiftfoot Boots
items.description: Polished boots with tiny warding buckles. They make every step feel hasty, protected, and just a little rules-lawyered.

items.name: Thousand Sunny deck shoes
items.description: Grippy ship shoes that hold steady on wet docks, mall tile, and any pier that suddenly decides it is a pirate ship.

items.name: Starter Duel token
items.description: A cheap plastic token stamped NORMAL SUMMON, good for one tiny boost of confidence before the real chain starts.

items.name: Starter Poke Ball clip
items.description: A red-and-white belt clip that rattles whenever your partner monster is about to sprint directly into responsibility.

items.name: Starter mana counter
items.description: A little spin-down counter set to one, because every Planeswalker deserves at least one clean source of Control.

items.name: Starter Haro patch
items.description: A cheerful round patch with a launch-check smile, built for pilots who need one more nudge toward the catapult rail.

items.name: Starter crew bracelet
items.description: A woven red bracelet tied like a promise: keep moving, protect the crew, and absolutely ignore the sensible route.

items.name: Starter AP badge
items.description: A cardboard Union Arena badge with one tiny AP pip, perfect for making the first assist feel official.

npcs.name: Marshal Echo
npcs.description: A volunteer marshal in a reflective vest, holding a clipboard like it can legally stop a Blue-Eyes White Dragon.

combat.defeatMessage: Marshal Echo blows a whistle and calls a timeout.

greeting: Welcome to Cardbound Plaza. If your Duel Disk starts glowing, point it away from traffic.

response: The mall turnstile needs a Duel Disk keycard. The card shop north kept the emergency one, because of course the card shop became important immediately.
Grab it, open the mall gate, and check on the kiosk kid inside before he starts issuing quests on receipt paper.

duelist: Your Duel Disk earns an approving glance. Key first, dramatic entrance second, Battle Phase strictly away from pedestrians.
trainer: Your Pokedex phone gets a quick tap. Treat the keycard like a Gym badge: earn access, keep moving, and do not let anything cute chew the scanner.
planeswalker: The turnstile is basically a one-mana artifact with terrible UX. Tap the keycard, resolve the gate, and avoid priority arguments.
pilot: Think of the mall gate as a catapult lock. Keycard in, lane clear, no indoor boost-dashing.
captain: Captain type, huh? Get the key, rally the plaza, and try not to declare the food court your ship.
arena-fighter: The gate wants a clean resource check. Spend the keycard, advance the board, and keep the crossover from snowballing.

response: At 3:14 PM every card game in town decided rules were optional and monsters were mandatory.
Good news: most of them respond to confidence. Bad news: confidence is not a Trap Card.

npcs.name: Nina Sleeves
npcs.description: A card shop owner with a price gun, a first-aid kit, and the terrifying calm of someone who has seen a midnight prerelease line.

combat.defeatMessage: Nina ducks behind a counter and declares you banned for six minutes.

greeting: Singles are half off if they are not currently alive. Please do not ask me to price the ceiling dragon.

response: Take the Duel Disk keycard if Marshal Echo sent you. Pay me back by keeping the mall from becoming a booster-pack volcano.
They are not evil. Mostly. They are like Pokemon, fireworks, and Magic judge calls all at once.

npcs.name: Kuriboh
npcs.description: A small, fuzzy Duel Monster flutters between the card tables, multiplying whenever danger gets too confident.

combat.defeatMessage: Kuriboh gives one brave squeak and dissolves into a swirl of harmless card-light.

greeting: Kuriboh fluffs up, determined to protect something much larger than itself.

card.flavor: A tiny shield with enough courage to stop a finishing blow.

npcs.name: Morphing Jar
npcs.description: A grinning stone jar rattles beneath the snack rack, eager to flip face-up and replace every careful plan with five new cards.

combat.defeatMessage: Morphing Jar snaps shut and rolls back beneath the display.

greeting: Morphing Jar wobbles ominously, daring someone to set it face-down.

card.flavor: One flip turns an orderly hand into a brand-new problem.

npcs.name: Signal DJ Pax
npcs.description: A teen DJ broadcasting monster alerts between jingles from behind a pretzel cart.

combat.defeatMessage: Pax drops the mic, catches it, and calls that a tactical retreat.

greeting: Cardbound Radio is live, improvised, and only a little on fire.

response: The roof mast needs a Rotom antenna sticker and a signal booster. Get the sticker from transit, the booster from the roof, then tell the technician we can go citywide.

duelist: If the broadcast glitches, call it Chain Link 2 and keep talking until the city believes you.
trainer: If Rotom starts editorializing, translate the important parts before it reviews everyone again.
planeswalker: If the signal splits into five colors, please identify which color means do not explode.
pilot: You are on antenna-launch supervision. If the mast says catapult, say absolutely not.
captain: A crew voice would help. The kind that makes a panicking mall believe help is already on the way.
arena-fighter: Guest-star warnings are your whole thing. Keep the callouts short enough to fit before the trigger resolves.

response: Every good emergency needs a theme song. I am taking requests after the flying vending machine lands.

npcs.name: Kiosk Kid Milo
npcs.description: A kid behind an information kiosk, armed with receipt paper, three markers, and terrifying Battle City logistics.

combat.defeatMessage: Milo ducks behind the kiosk and writes COMBAT IS NOT CUSTOMER SERVICE.

greeting: I have maps, coupons, and an evolving theory that the escalators are unionizing.

response: The mall is weird but navigable. Arcade east, Magic lab through service, Gunpla aisle west. If a sign says Final Sale, do not duel it.
Tell the marshal I am alive, helpful, and only charging one coupon per rescue.

npcs.name: Goblin of Greed
npcs.description: The famously avaricious goblin has armored itself in expired coupons and refuses to let anyone pass without paying imaginary interest.

combat.defeatMessage: Goblin of Greed drops its coupon ledger and scrambles after the loose change.

greeting: Goblin of Greed thrusts out one palm and names a completely unreasonable toll.

card.flavor: It can appraise any treasure except the value of restraint.

npcs.name: Charizard
npcs.description: A Charizard prowls between arcade cabinets, its tail flame reflected in a hundred high-score screens.

combat.defeatMessage: Charizard folds its wings and settles beside the warm arcade vents.

greeting: Charizard rumbles a challenge as the nearest cabinet starts playing boss music.

card.flavor: Its tail flame makes every trip through the Game Corner look like a bonus stage.

npcs.name: Roof Technician Bea
npcs.description: A technician zip-tied to a safety harness and absolutely done with magical static.

combat.defeatMessage: Bea points to the safety sign until you feel bad.

greeting: If this mast explodes into confetti, I am invoicing the entire concept of card games.

response: Sticker plus booster equals citywide warning. Probably. If the warning comes out as a guitar solo, that is still useful.

npcs.name: Fan Rotom
npcs.description: Rotom has possessed the rooftop ventilation fan and is gleefully scrambling every broadcast with Electric- and Flying-type interference.

combat.defeatMessage: Fan Rotom spins down, chirps through the radio mast, and restores the signal.

telegraphs.name: Forecast Overcharge
telegraphs.warning: {name} pulls lightning into the broadcast fan. Break the charge before it discharges!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} spins into a crackling Forecast Overcharge.
telegraphs.counterHint: Deal 8 damage before the countdown ends
telegraphs.successMessage: The concentrated hits knock {name} out of the airflow and cancel Forecast Overcharge!
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} floods the rooftop with a burst of charged wind.

greeting: Fan Rotom crackles: "Forecast: one hundred percent chance of mischief!"

card.flavor: A rooftop Rotom whose weather report is mostly static, gusts, and laughter.

npcs.name: Professor Amper
npcs.description: A researcher in safety goggles, holding a Mox prism shard with barbecue tongs.

combat.defeatMessage: Professor Amper retreats behind a trifold display.

greeting: Excellent, a test subject. I mean helper. I said helper.

response: The Magic leak is sorting monsters by color identity and mood. Bring me a Mox prism shard, then check the vault before the colors start voting.

duelist: If the prism sets a face-down enchantment, do not ask why. Just remember where the trap line is.
trainer: If the prism identifies as every type at once, smile politely and do not throw a ball at it.
planeswalker: Finally, someone who can say color identity without everyone leaving the table.
pilot: If the lab starts beeping, assume it is not a launch cue.
captain: If a shard tries to join your crew, tell it probation starts after containment.
arena-fighter: The prism is producing resources in the wrong phase. Please make that sound normal.

response: Science is what we call it when screaming becomes repeatable and the stack finally resolves.

npcs.name: Colossal Dreadmaw
npcs.description: Colossal Dreadmaw crashes through the bulk bins with the confidence of six power, six toughness, and trample.

combat.defeatMessage: Colossal Dreadmaw collapses into a neatly sorted stack of green commons.

greeting: Colossal Dreadmaw roars. Somewhere, a limited player nods knowingly.

card.flavor: The convention learned why six mana and trample still deserve a clear aisle.

npcs.name: Fusion Elemental
npcs.description: Fusion Elemental towers over the mana displays, five colors of raw magic barely agreeing on a single silhouette.

combat.defeatMessage: Fusion Elemental separates into five calm streams of mana and sinks into the color pie.

greeting: Fusion Elemental flares white, blue, black, red, and green in rapid succession.

card.flavor: Five colors combine into one enormous reason to fix your mana base.

npcs.name: Ranger Mira
npcs.description: A park ranger with bite-proof gloves and a whistle labeled PLEASE WORK.

combat.defeatMessage: Ranger Mira blows the whistle and everyone takes five.

greeting: Approach slowly, speak kindly, and never assume cute means harmless.

response: Grab Poke Ball rescue tags from the ranger table or arcade, tag the park sightings, and report back before the little ones discover the hot dog cart.

duelist: Set your traps gently. We are rescuing the little troublemakers, not sending them to the Shadow Realm.
trainer: You already know the rhythm: approach, offer snacks, praise the good choice, dodge the tail.
planeswalker: Please avoid summoning anything larger than the picnic shelter unless absolutely necessary.
pilot: Low profile. The small ones spook when someone starts narrating a launch sequence.
captain: Your job is morale. Tell them the crew is safe, then mean it.
arena-fighter: No raid calls unless the whole group is ready. That includes the plush ones.

npcs.name: Charmander
npcs.description: A bright-eyed Charmander naps inside an oversized card sleeve, its tail flame safely poking out the open end.

combat.defeatMessage: Charmander gives a tired chirp and accepts the rescue tag.

greeting: Charmander hops up and guards its little patch of warm pavement.

card.flavor: Its tail flame turns an ordinary card sleeve into the coziest sleeping bag in the park.

npcs.name: Haro Tutorial Dummy
npcs.description: A round green Haro has commandeered a foam training body and a screen that says PRESS A TO PRACTICE RESPONSIBLY.

combat.defeatMessage: Haro awards you a B-plus, detaches from the dummy, and rolls away.

greeting: Haro chirps: "READY! PRACTICE! HARO!"

card.flavor: A cheerful training partner with professional-grade feedback and squeaks.

npcs.name: Mechanic Joss
npcs.description: A mechanic surrounded by runners, sprues, decals, and tiny parts sorted with terrifying precision.

combat.defeatMessage: Joss lectures you about tool safety until combat becomes impossible.

greeting: Do not touch the red launch button. The blue one is also bad. Actually, hands in pockets.

response: The White Base hangar door needs the Gunpla servo key from the training lot. Get in, check the catapult rail, and stop the plastic mecha before it discovers stairs.

duelist: If the Gunpla declares an attack, please remind it that model kits do not get Main Phase Two indoors.
trainer: Talk to the Haro first. The mecha listens to it more than to me, which is upsetting but useful.
planeswalker: If you can counterspell a catapult rail, now would be a beautiful time.
pilot: Finally. Servo key, hangar door, target lock. Please pretend the safety briefing was longer.
captain: Keep the crew back from the launch line. Yes, even if someone yells adventure.
arena-fighter: If the frame raids into a bigger frame, I am walking into the ocean.

npcs.name: Full Armor Gundam
npcs.description: The Full Armor Gundam fills the hangar lane with layered armor, twin beam rifles, and enough missile pods to make the safety officer hide.

combat.defeatMessage: Full Armor Gundam vents its remaining propellant and powers down beneath the catapult lights.

telegraphs.name: Full Armor Barrage
telegraphs.warning: Targeting lines sweep across the lane as {name} opens every missile pod!
telegraphs.counterHint: Use a skill that grants Guard before the barrage launches
telegraphs.successMessage: Your defensive play spoils the missile lock and forces {name} to abort the barrage!
telegraphs.failureMessage: {name} unleashes Full Armor Barrage for {damage} damage.
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} fills the hangar with a disciplined missile barrage.

phases.name: Armor Purged
phases.description: The Gundam trades protection for a much faster attack cycle.
phases.enterMessage: {name} ejects its damaged armor and surges forward with the weight gone!

greeting: Full Armor Gundam locks both beam rifles forward as every missile hatch clicks open.

card.flavor: Armor, thrusters, rifles, and missiles turn a display model into a one-unit fleet.

npcs.name: Captain Rook
npcs.description: A waterfront volunteer wearing a captain hat over a bike helmet and taking crew safety very seriously.

combat.defeatMessage: Captain Rook calls a snack break and refuses to duel volunteers.

greeting: Welcome aboard the pier, which is legally still a sidewalk.

response: Grab Straw Hat tokens from the harbor or pier. The rubber-band brute respects bold declarations and also snacks. Mostly bold declarations.
The pavilion is where everything crashes together. Bring a Union Arena pass unless you enjoy arguing with sentient badge scanners.

npcs.name: Monkey D. Luffy
npcs.description: Monkey D. Luffy bounds along the harbor with impossible rubber limbs, a straw hat, and absolute certainty that the next fight will be fun.

combat.defeatMessage: Luffy rebounds into a stack of barrels, laughs, and declares the rematch a feast.

greeting: Luffy winds up a punch from far outside sensible melee range.

card.flavor: Distance is only a suggestion when your captain fights like rubber.

npcs.name: Coordinator Vale
npcs.description: An event coordinator with six lanyards, seven schedules, and no remaining fear.

combat.defeatMessage: Vale reschedules the fight for never.

greeting: If one more universe enters from stage left, I am billing overtime to destiny.

response: Take the Union Arena pass, get it through the stage gate, and stop the genre jam in the pavilion before every rulebook merges into soup.

duelist: If the finale starts monologuing, interrupt politely with the largest dragon you can legally justify.
trainer: Call the targets like Gym leaders. The crowd calms down when the plan has badge energy.
planeswalker: If you can planeswalk the rules back into separate sleeves, I will name a panel after you.
pilot: You are on big-silhouette duty. Anything taller than the stage truss gets a beam-saber warning.
captain: If the finale tries to swallow the harbor, give it the kind of speech that makes physics embarrassed.
arena-fighter: This is your big moment and your home turf. Track the triggers, spend the AP, and keep the guests from overrunning the show.

npcs.name: Mahito
npcs.description: Mahito steps out of a Jujutsu Kaisen Union Arena card, smiling as the shape of his soul refuses to stay still.

combat.defeatMessage: Mahito recoils into his card frame, his distorted silhouette snapping flat.

greeting: Mahito studies your soul with delighted, deeply unwelcome curiosity.

card.flavor: A Special Trigger is considerably less comforting when a cursed spirit is holding it.

npcs.name: Final Trigger Titan
npcs.description: A city-sized finale avatar assembled from Duel Monsters, Pokemon, Magic, Gundam, One Piece, and Union Arena card frames.

combat.defeatMessage: The Final Trigger Titan splits into six exhausted card frames and one very relieved city.

telegraphs.name: Six-Series Cataclysm
telegraphs.warning: {name} aligns six card frames into a single finishing beam!
telegraphs.roomWarning: Six franchise emblems lock into orbit around {name}.
telegraphs.counterHint: Deal 10 damage to break the alignment
telegraphs.successMessage: The barrage cracks the card-frame alignment and Six-Series Cataclysm fizzles!
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} fires six overlapping finishers across the pavilion.

telegraphs.name: Final Trigger Lock
telegraphs.warning: {name} seals the action stack and starts counting down a Final Trigger!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} clamps a Final Trigger over the pavilion.
telegraphs.counterHint: Spend 2 class-mechanic stacks on a skill
telegraphs.successMessage: Your signature technique tears open the action stack and cancels Final Trigger Lock!
telegraphs.failureMessage: {name} completes Final Trigger Lock for {damage} damage.
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} drops a completed Final Trigger across the battlefield.

phases.name: Pages Collide
phases.description: The Titan accelerates as all six binder pages overlap.
phases.enterMessage: The binder pages around {name} collide, sharpening every attack!

phases.name: Final Trigger
phases.description: The finale enters its fastest and most dangerous last act.
phases.enterMessage: {name} tears away the last safe card frame. FINAL TRIGGER!

greeting: The Final Trigger Titan raises six opening themes at once.

card.flavor: Every featured game compressed into one impossible final card.

npcs.name: Winged Kuriboh LV10
npcs.description: A radiant Winged Kuriboh LV10 hovers above the street, every feather shining like a secret rare.

card.flavor: Transcendent Wings turns a tiny guardian into a brilliant last resort.

combat.defeatMessage: Winged Kuriboh LV10 folds its luminous wings and returns to a premium sleeve.

greeting: Winged Kuriboh LV10 shines brightly enough to turn traffic into a dramatic reveal.

npcs.name: Shiny Charmander
npcs.description: A golden Shiny Charmander patrols the park, its blue-white tail flame reflecting off every rescue tag.

card.flavor: A rare golden Charmander whose tail flame makes every rescue look like a special event.

combat.defeatMessage: Shiny Charmander chirps, accepts a rescue tag, and settles beside its ordinary-colored friend.

greeting: Shiny Charmander poses proudly as its unusual coloring catches the sun.

npcs.name: Mega Charizard X
npcs.description: Mega Charizard X looms above the arcade cabinet, black scales and blue fire framed by a flashing bonus-stage border.

card.flavor: Blue fire and a Mega Stone turn the high-score cabinet into a Dragon-type boss arena.

combat.defeatMessage: Mega Charizard X drops out of Mega Evolution as the cabinet flashes GAME CLEAR.

greeting: Mega Charizard X roars through the arcade speakers in sixteen-bit surround sound.

npcs.name: Guren Mk-II
npcs.description: The crimson Guren Mk-II tears out of a Code Geass Union Arena display, its right hand glowing with Radiant Wave energy.

card.flavor: A crimson Knightmare Frame whose right hand can end both armor and arguments.

combat.defeatMessage: Guren Mk-II plants its feet, vents crimson energy, and powers down.

greeting: Guren Mk-II raises its Radiant Wave Surger as the preview-stage lights turn red.

npcs.name: Performage Trick Clown
npcs.description: Performage Trick Clown cartwheels across the sidewalk, turning every face-down card into part of a dangerous stage routine.

card.flavor: Every pratfall is rehearsed; every damage trigger is part of the act.

combat.defeatMessage: Performage Trick Clown takes a theatrical bow and vanishes through a painted trapdoor.

greeting: Performage Trick Clown tosses three cards into the air and lands on the least stable one.

npcs.name: Mimikyu
npcs.description: Mimikyu shuffles through the park in its handmade Pikachu disguise, guarding every crooked stitch with fierce devotion.

card.flavor: A lonely Ghost-type whose brave disguise deserves kindness and a respectful distance.

combat.defeatMessage: Mimikyu steadies its disguise and retreats into the shade beneath the prize booth.

greeting: Mimikyu tilts its cloth head, then its shadow moves a moment too early.

npcs.name: Usopp
npcs.description: Usopp guards the pier with a slingshot, a pouch of Pop Greens, and several heroic stories growing taller by the second.

card.flavor: A brave warrior of the sea, especially once the danger is several rooftops away.

combat.defeatMessage: Usopp performs a tactical retreat, loudly assuring the harbor it was all according to plan.

greeting: Usopp announces that eight thousand followers are waiting just out of sight.

npcs.name: Porygon
npcs.description: Porygon flickers out of the prize terminal and rolls through the arcade as crisp polygons and cheerful error tones.

card.flavor: A virtual Pokemon equally at home in cyberspace and a malfunctioning prize counter.

combat.defeatMessage: Porygon compresses into a tidy data packet and returns to the prize terminal.

greeting: Porygon chirps in modem tones as its edges cycle through red, blue, and yellow.

npcs.name: Gon Freecss
npcs.description: Gon Freecss bounds out of a Hunter x Hunter Union Arena display, fascinated by every model kit and completely ready for a challenge.

card.flavor: A guest character whose simple punch carries an extraordinary amount of resolve.

combat.defeatMessage: Gon lowers his fist, grins at the lesson, and hops back into the guest display.

greeting: Gon plants his feet and starts charging Jajanken with alarming enthusiasm.

npcs.name: Post-Credits Colossus
npcs.description: A towering encore monster made of scrolling credits, recap cards, and one last impossible pose.

card.flavor: An encore card printed after the ending because the city still had confetti left.

combat.defeatMessage: The Credits Colossus scrolls past SPECIAL THANKS and finally sits down.

greeting: The Post-Credits Colossus raises a glowing card that says AND ONE MORE THING.

npcs.name: Blue-Eyes White Dragon
npcs.description: Blue-Eyes White Dragon blocks the turnstiles in a blaze of white scales and overwhelming attack power.

combat.defeatMessage: Blue-Eyes White Dragon folds its wings and dissolves into glittering cardstock.

specials.name: Burst Stream of Destruction
specials.message: {name} fires Burst Stream of Destruction through the turnstiles for {damage} damage.
specials.roomMessage: {name} lights the turnstiles with Burst Stream of Destruction.

greeting: Blue-Eyes White Dragon lowers its head as the intersection fills with boss music.

card.flavor: A legendary dragon whose power remains excessive even during rush hour.

npcs.name: Misty's Gyarados
npcs.description: Misty's Gyarados coils through the park pond, protecting the rescue route with the intensity of a Cerulean Gym match.

combat.defeatMessage: Misty's Gyarados gives a grudging nod and sinks back into the pond.

telegraphs.name: Hyper Beam
telegraphs.warning: {name}'s mouth fills with white light. Establish a guard before Hyper Beam fires!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} begins charging Hyper Beam over the pond.
telegraphs.counterHint: Use a skill that grants Guard
telegraphs.successMessage: Your guard redirects the charge and forces {name} to cancel Hyper Beam!
telegraphs.failureMessage: {name} fires Hyper Beam for {damage} damage, then recoils to recharge.
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} sends Hyper Beam blazing across the park path.

phases.name: Raging Current
phases.description: The pond churns, slightly increasing Gyarados speed and power.
phases.enterMessage: The pond becomes a raging current around {name}!

greeting: Misty's Gyarados rises from the pond and roars a formal Gym challenge.

card.flavor: A Cerulean powerhouse testing whether rescue volunteers can keep calm around a real Gym ace.

npcs.name: Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God
npcs.description: Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God steps through the animated display, drawing five colors of mana into a scheme already several turns ahead.

combat.defeatMessage: Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God retreats through the Meditation Realm portal, insisting defeat was part of the plan.

telegraphs.name: Cruel Ultimatum
telegraphs.warning: {name} begins assembling Cruel Ultimatum. Break his concentration!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} draws seven mana into the colors of Cruel Ultimatum.
telegraphs.counterHint: Deal 9 damage before the spell resolves
telegraphs.successMessage: Your pressure breaks concentration and sends Cruel Ultimatum to the graveyard!

phases.name: Dragon-God Ascendant
phases.description: Bolas seizes the power of every planeswalker represented in the display.
phases.enterMessage: {name} spreads his wings and claims every loyalty ability on the field!

greeting: Nicol Bolas, Dragon-God demands priority and the immediate surrender of every spark in the room.

card.flavor: An Elder Dragon planeswalker with every loyalty ability and none of the humility.

npcs.name: Char's Zaku II
npcs.description: Char's red Zaku II circles the catapult rail at three times the expected speed, heat hawk ready.

combat.defeatMessage: Char's Zaku II skids to a controlled stop and salutes beneath the hangar lights.

telegraphs.name: Heat Hawk Charge
telegraphs.warning: {name} lowers its shoulder and superheats the hawk. Stop the charge with focused damage!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} lines up a three-times-faster Heat Hawk Charge.
telegraphs.counterHint: Deal 10 damage before the charge connects
telegraphs.successMessage: Your hits knock the heat hawk wide and send {name} skidding past!
telegraphs.failureMessage: {name} completes Heat Hawk Charge for {damage} damage.
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} carves a red arc through the catapult lane.

phases.name: Red Comet Acceleration
phases.description: The Zaku begins attacking faster as Char commits to the duel.
phases.enterMessage: {name} kicks its thrusters higher and becomes a red blur!

phases.name: Three Times Faster
phases.description: The final attack cycle is dramatically faster and more dangerous.
phases.enterMessage: {name} sheds every limiter. Three times faster!

greeting: Char's Zaku II blurs past the warning tape and raises its heat hawk.

card.flavor: A commander-type red mobile suit whose speed makes scale-model safety rules irrelevant.

npcs.name: Dracule Mihawk
npcs.description: Dracule Mihawk waits at the end of the pier with Yoru resting across one shoulder, turning an optional duel into a test of nerve.

combat.defeatMessage: Dracule Mihawk lowers Yoru, gives one measured nod, and allows the challenger to pass.

telegraphs.name: Observation Counter
telegraphs.warning: {name} becomes perfectly still, reading your next attack. Brace instead of taking the bait!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} raises Yoru into a silent counter stance.
telegraphs.counterHint: Brace before the counter window closes
telegraphs.successMessage: You refuse the opening. {name} lowers Yoru, and the counter stance passes harmlessly.
telegraphs.failureMessage: {name} reads your commitment and answers with a black-blade counter for {damage} damage.
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} answers an opening with one immaculate black-blade stroke.

phases.name: Measured Seriously
phases.description: Mihawk acknowledges the challenger and tightens his timing.
phases.enterMessage: {name} shifts to a two-handed grip. The duel has earned his attention.

greeting: Dracule Mihawk watches in silence, waiting to see whether you strike or lose your nerve.

card.flavor: One calm swing from the black blade can divide ships, ambitions, and careless challengers.

npcs.name: Meruem
npcs.description: Meruem stands beneath the Union Arena lights with royal composure, analyzing every AP counter and every technique used against him.

combat.defeatMessage: Meruem concedes the match in silence and steps back into the Hunter x Hunter card frame.

telegraphs.name: Royal Adaptation
telegraphs.warning: {name} begins adapting to your ordinary attacks. Commit your class mechanic to a signature play!
telegraphs.roomWarning: {name} reads every repeated movement and begins to adapt.
telegraphs.counterHint: Spend 2 class-mechanic stacks on a skill
telegraphs.successMessage: Your signature play breaks the pattern and overwhelms Royal Adaptation!
telegraphs.failureMessage: {name} completes Royal Adaptation and punishes the predictable line for {damage} damage.
telegraphs.roomFailureMessage: {name} adapts to the duel and answers with royal precision.

phases.name: Post-Rose Evolution
phases.description: Meruem returns stronger, faster, and even more perceptive.
phases.enterMessage: {name} emerges from the smoke transformed, his aura filling the entire stage!

greeting: Meruem studies your stance once and appears to understand it better than you do.

card.flavor: The Chimera Ant King adapts to every play until only a truly personal technique can surprise him.
